category_title: Students motivated by high scores who restart tasks when challenged
scenario_title: Encountering challenging math sums on a handheld console
minimal_context: The student is playing the x20 math game on a Nintendo DS to practice single-digit calculations.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's start the x20 game on your Nintendo DS. Go ahead and answer the math problems.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the screen and uses his fingers to count) Got it. (Writes the answer with the stylus)

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks away from the screen, appearing bored and distracted) I don't know this one.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Just try to figure it out.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: This sum is too hard, I'm not going to get the highest score. (Reaches over and resets the DS console)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's start the x20 game on your Nintendo DS. Go ahead and answer the math problems.

STUDENT: (Looks at the screen and uses his fingers to count) Got it. (Writes the answer with the stylus)

TUTOR: Do the next one.

STUDENT: (Looks away from the screen, appearing bored and distracted) I don't know this one.

TUTOR: Just try to figure it out.

STUDENT: This sum is too hard, I'm not going to get the highest score. (Reaches over and resets the DS console)

task.content: Playing the x20 game (random single digit addition, subtraction and multiplication) on a Nintendo DS using a stylus
task.learning_goal: solving single digit addition, subtraction and multiplication problems

context.materials: Nintendo DS console, Dr Kawashima’s Brain Training software, stylus, rulers, number lines

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: When the sums became challenging and he felt he was not going to do well
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Restarting the game or choosing to reset the DS rather than persisting and working out the correct answer
challenging_behavior_trigger.internal_state: Motivated by getting the highest score possible; reluctance to persist

behavior_patterns.condition: When unable to do any mental math calculations correctly
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Appeared bored, was easily distracted, and needed to be consistently prompted by the EA to complete the sums
behavior_patterns.internal_state: Had not yet learned to work independently
